issue_title: Even if the format is a string it is displayed as a float
issue_body:
This a bug of display and very annoying, since even if a number (like serial number for example) is wirtten as a text into a table it is still displayed as a float, 

Example:

'>>> ReportWorkbook = pe.get_book(file_name=rep_name)
'>>> ReportWorkbook['Infos']
Sheet Name: Infos
+-----------------+------------------+
| Date            | 2016-03-31 10:59 |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Model name      | XS360_EU        |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Time by X     | 60               |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Product A       | 123              |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Product B       | 4.567e+15        |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Bandwidth (UDP) | N/A              |
+-----------------+------------------+
| Other infos     | N/A              |
+-----------------+------------------+
'>>> ReportWorkbook['Infos'].column[1]
['2016-03-31 10:59', 'XS360_EU', 60.0, '0123', '04566651561653122', 'N/A', 'N/A']

As seen  above the serial numbers of products A and B are 0123 and 04566651561653122 are stocked as a text (string), however. Whend displayed with print they are displayed as a float so we se the first '0' disappear and 4.567e+15 insetead of 04566651561653122

issue_title: How to define a custom filter and bind it to a resource endpoint
issue_body:
I'm using Python EVE, EVE-SQLALchemy, Flask, Flask-SQLALchemy, SQLALchemy and a Postgres database.

I have my API endpoints all setup and they are working fine out the box.

Now i'm trying to filter the items returned by the _some_model_ resource so that it only returns items (GET) that belong to a certain user (the user doing the API request) or have a certain name (prefix). The SQlAlcehmy code to perform this is below:

```
session.query(SomeModel).filter(or_(SomeModel.name.like('SOMETHING_%'), SomeModel.account_id==1)).all()
```

I've looked at pre and post request hooks, User-Restricted Resource Access and everything else I could find, but nowhere seems to describe how to apply custom filters to (all) GET requests of a certain endpoint/resource. 

Kind of like the URL filter functionality, but always on and filtering at the database level, not the serialization/response level.

issue_body:
Set verbose_name flag in resource class. If True verbose_name is used from Django model's field definition. If no verbose_name field is explicitly defined in the model Django' set this value to the default field_name.

	class JiraResource(resources.ModelResource):

		class Meta:
			model = JiraRequest
			import_id_fields = ('key',)
			exclude = ('id',)
			skip_unchanged = True

Patch:
	--- a/import_export/resources.py
	+++ b/import_export/resources.py
	@@ -124,6 +124,12 @@ class ResourceOptions(object):
		 Controls if the result reports skipped rows Default value is True
		 """

	+    verbose_name = False
	+    """
	+    Controls if column name used from Django's model verbose_name attribute. Default value is False
	+    """
	+
	+

	 class DeclarativeMetaclass(type):

	@@ -742,11 +748,15 @@ class ModelResource(six.with_metaclass(ModelDeclarativeMetaclass, Resource)):
			 Returns a Resource Field instance for the given Django model field.
			 """

	+        column_name = field_name
	+        if self._meta.verbose_name:

issue_title: get_field_values_as_list for empty fields
issue_body:
Hello,
I have recently worked with your package, and noticed that it has a somewhat strange behaviour when encountering fields that are only present in some of the documents.
In my example, I had let's say 2500 documents with an unique "id" field, but only 2000 or so would have another field called "links".
Iterating over both the "id" list and "links" list is not possible, since they have different lengths, since `get_field_values_as_list("links")` would return only a list of length 2000, and I cannot discern which pages return empty.
For this, I simply altered the function to:
`    return [doc[field] if field in doc else [] for doc in self.docs]`
which solved the problem in my case. It might not always be needed, but possibly this could be included as `get_field_values_as_full_list`, or something like this.

Best regards,
Dennis

issue_title: Argument for ignoring certain files with the detect-private-key hook
issue_body:
It would be great if the pre-commit hook for detecting private keys could be passed an argument for files to ignore, something like `--ignored-files`.  My use case is having private keys in my testing code, in particular, testing asymmetric jwt decryption.

I can skip individual rules on a per-commit basis (using `SKIP`), but parts of my build pipeline run all files through the pre-commit hooks to check for errors. As far as I know, there is no way in pre-commit to specify files that are globally ignored. 

It would be a bonus if the hook trimmed the whitespace of the args after splitting them, so they can be pretty printed in a yml file:
```
hooks:
-   id: detect-private-key
    args:
    - --ignored-files
    - __tests__/my-test.secret.key,
      __tests__/my-other-test.secret.key
```

issue_title: Adaptive Softmax
issue_body:
Hey!

Thanks for this awesome Pytorch project! I'm totally sold on the idea, and wanted to take a crack and getting involved! 

I found recently that for language model training where the output vocabulary space is high-dimensional, that Adaptive Softmax from (https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.04309) can lead to a huge speed up. It's essentially a modification of hierarchical softmax that is optimized for GPU computing, and it can sometimes lead to orders of magnitude speed improvements. I figured that since this would make seq2seq decoding a lot faster, it'd perhaps be useful, not to mention a good way for me to dive in and find my way around. Does that sound helpful? If not a have a few other features I want to implement so I could propose something else. 

If that sounds good, I got the code from this repo: https://github.com/rosinality/adaptive-softmax-pytorch, to work with the word_language_model example in Pytorch, and thought it'd be a good place to start. I figured that it'd make sense to implement the output projection, activation, and loss in an nn module, and then add an option for seq2seq training to use this activation and loss rather than the normal seq2seq and crossentropy. Does that sound good?

Also I noticed in the seq2seq training file that the decoder is hardcoded as an LSTM, but is intended to implemented differently by @pdasigi. If you don't mind my asking, what's the plan for that? I want to use the Transformer seq2seq which requires changing the decoder.

issue_title: Header errors in pyregion 2.0
issue_body:
A year ago, I wrote a tutorial showing how to use pyregion for simple aperture photometry. It worked then, but now the same code breaks.    

Here's the tutorial:  https://github.com/janerigby/NB/blob/master/Example%20pyds9%20and%20pyregion.ipynb

The error seems to be from get_mask():  "ValueError: header must be an instance of pyfits.Header or astropy.io.fits.Header".  

Below is a an awkward workaround using get_filter() and then mask(), but I'd really like to solve the underlying issue -- why pyregion doesn't like the fits header.
```
import numpy as np
from astropy.io import fits
from astropy.utils.data import download_file
import pyregion

# Here is a short example showing what breaks.
image_file = download_file('http://data.astropy.org/tutorials/FITS-images/HorseHead.fits', cache=True )
f = fits.open(image_file)
dum = f[0].data  # screwing around to get hdu as pyregions wants it
im = dum.astype(np.float64)
reg = 'image;circle(727.0,603.0,21)' # example region
object_region = pyregion.parse(reg)
total_flux = np.nansum(object_region.get_mask(f[0]) * im)   # get_mask breaks, complains of header

#This is my awkward workaround, using get_filter() and then mask()
myfilter = object_region.get_filter()
total_flux = np.nansum(myfilter.mask(im) * im)  #nansum treats nans as zeros for summation
```

issue_title: dependency conflict with but no conflict listed in graph

If I have both `pytest` and `pytest-watch` in my Pipfile I get a complaint that "Could not find a version that matches pluggy<0.7,>=0.5,>=0.7" when running pipenv install

issue_title: Missing hashes when using private PyPI
issue_body:
Pipenvs behavior used to be to leave out hashes on private PyPIs. Then it introduced them (11.1.7 works fine), now it broke them: running pipenv update removes most of the hashes from the lock file.

My suspicion is that it computes the hashes for the packages it uses itself or something (I think I saw some code in my last debugging session :))? In any case that doesn’t seem useful for people who deploy to different platforms than they develop on (= majority if I’m not mistaken).

So I would argue to either fix it again or have no hashes at all. The current status is probably breaking deployments somewhere. ;)

issue_title: AlignedSegment hash logic is faulty
issue_body:
From the Python [docs](https://docs.python.org/2/reference/datamodel.html#object.__hash__):
> \_\_hash\_\_() should return an integer. The only required property is that objects which compare equal have the same hash value

This is a source of erratic behaviour and extremely hard to track bugs. For example:
`al_seq in my_dict` -> `False`
`al_seq in my_dict.keys()` -> `True`

issue_title: gradient on einsum doesn't handle broadcasting appropriately
issue_body:
```python
x = anp.array([2., 2.])
y = anp.array([2.])

def f(x):
    return anp.einsum("i, i", x, y)
```
```python
>>> grad(f, 0)(x).shape
(1,)
```

This is cause by the fact that the backpropagation, `dfdx = einsum(",i", 1, y)`, utilizes `i=1`, whereas i should represent 2. Fortunately, this can be remedied trivially by broadcasting `dfdx` to the shape of `x`.

issue_title: IndexError using client.entry when no entry with given id exists
issue_body:
This will throw a somewhat confusing IndexError when no entry with the id exists.
```python
client.entry('1234')
```

This is because the entry function assumes something will always exist and tries to return the first item in the result.
```python
return self._get(
    '/entries',
    query
)[0]
```

I propose adding a more descriptive exception, i.e. ```EntryNotFound```. If you like this idea I can submit a PR for it.

issue_title: synchronous producer hangs when kafka is unreachable
issue_body:
_This issue was brought to my attention by Michael Kowalchik in the [mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pykafka-user/iyZlHj_NTDU)_

**Steps to reproduce**
- Run a synchronous producer pointing at a kafka cluster with a single node
- Kill the single kafka node while the producer is running
- Observe that the producer's main thread hangs and must be killed externally

The expected result is that the producer's main thread should raise a `SocketDisconnectedError` from `produce()`.

The following sequence of events causes this bug. When a broker is unavailable, `SocketDisconnectedError` is raised and caught. If there are no brokers available, [this call to _update()](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L366) raises an [exception](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/cluster.py#L286). This in turn [kills the worker thread](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L443), meaning that code after the `_update` call, including pushing the original `SocketDisconnectedError` onto the delivery reports queue, does not execute. On the main thread, `produce()` is still waiting for the [delivery report](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/producer.py#L268), which will never come because the worker has died.

Possible solutions include ignoring certain exception types from the `_update()` call, or catching any exception that happens between `to_retry` being populated and the delivery report being enqueued. I'd lean toward simply catching and warning about [this specific exception](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.1.2/pykafka/cluster.py#L286), maybe giving it a more specific name in the process.

issue_title: Importing exported inventory with one host in two inventories and groups throws association error
NgramWordAnalyzer and PorterStemmer are not independent?
issue_body:
Based on feedback from CS410 instructor  Ismini Lourentzou I am reporting the following issue regarding the interaction of Ngrams and Stemmers in MeTA.

Briefly: should they not be ***_independent_** ?  In that case the  **order** should not matter.

However in MeTA the order does result in different results - consider:

    stemtok = metapy.analyzers.Porter2Filter(previousTokenizer)
    stemtok.set_content(doc.content())
    ng3ana = metapy.analyzers.NGramWordAnalyzer(3,stemtok)
    character_trigrams = ng3ana.analyze(doc)

The above yields slightly different results than the following:
    
      ng3ana = metapy.analyzers.NGramWordAnalyzer(3,stemtok)
      character_trigrams = ng3ana.analyze(doc)
      stemtok.set_content(doc.content())

Notice in the second case that the stemmer has occurred *after* the NGram. 

To better understand the intended concept of independence vs dependence -   consider a different pair of analyzers that *should* be dependent: the  stemmer and the  length analyzer. Swapping *those* analyzers that would definitely impact the result: e.g.  the stem of derivation and derived should be the same - maybe deriv ?  In that case if the length<=5 rule were applied before  the stemming then the word would disappear altogether: but after stemming it is retained.
 
But I do not perceive any fundamental reason why stemming and ngram generation should have any mutual effect /interactions: they should be independent,  no?  If they were independent then order should not matter.  But it does here. So then can we get some insight from the developer(s) why these two features actually do interact - and why?

issue_title: Makefile - a user extension
Implement Client Credentials Authorizer
issue_body:
I was working on an example of the Client Credentials grant when I realized that this is a possibility.

The `RefreshTokenAuthorizer` tracks expiration time and automatically requests new tokens for the user.
However, there are no automated utilities for managing tokens fetched via Client Credentials grants.

It occurred to me that we could define a `ClientCredentialsAuthorizer` built from a `ConfidentialAppAuthClient` and a set of scopes.
The scopes are necessary in order to specify what resource server (and scopes against it) are used -- the authorizer can fetch and store access tokens via the client credentials grant, always choosing the top-level token.
We could even have it error-out if `other_tokens` are returned (i.e. bad scopes supplied).

Does this have value? Would it help client authors?

issue_title: Need a way to limit threads per remote
issue_body: We have an OLAP application on Pyramid + Waitress; and we are using AJAX to fetch the results. Sometimes the computations are not that swift (especially if PostgreSQL makes a bad query plan); the problem is that 1 client doing the lengthy ops, or reloading the page could easily exhaust all the worker threads. There ought to be way to configure a policy whether a request is dispatched on yet another thread, or queued until the previous requests from that remote address are fulfilled.

issue_title: pyramid_tm broken on Python 2 with transaction 2.0
issue_body:
Transaction 2.0 defined the transaction note, username and description to all be text (unicode on Python 2.0), but pyramid_tm passes `native_` values (meaning str/bytes on Python 2). This leads directly to TypeError (prior to 2.0.3, it could lead to UnicodeError in some cases).  (See https://github.com/zopefoundation/transaction/issues/34 for a specific stack trace.)

I'm willing to offer a PR to address this.

Hi,

What I am trying to do is to use lstm on IMDB data for sentiment analysis by updating the code in following link https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/examples/nlp/lstm.py

However it uses a preprocessed data, but I wanna use my own IMDB raw data (downloaded from http://ai.stanford.edu/~amaas/data/sentiment/)

Here is the code that I updated for the sentiment analysis, all the intermediate steps seem correct but the accuracy is not stable (as you can see results below). When I print the predictions at the end, I see that the probabilities for each class is very very close (like _[[0.4999946355819702, 0.5000053644180298], [0.5000001192092896, 0.49999988079071045], [0.49999362230300903, 0.5000064373016357], [0.49999985098838806, 0.5000001192092896]])_.

I don't think that the problem is overfitting, since when I try to predict train data again, the result is as above. I think I am missing some point or doing something wrong.

Any help is appreciated, Thanks

```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

import tflearn
from tflearn.data_utils import to_categorical, pad_sequences
import string
import numpy as nm
import codecs
import re
import collections
import math
import tensorflow as tf
import random

allWords = []
allDocuments = []
allLabels = []

def readFile(fileName, allWords):


    file = codecs.open(fileName, encoding='utf-8')

    for line in file:
        line = line.lower().encode('utf-8')
        words = line.split()
        for word in words:
            word = word.translate(None, string.punctuation)
            if word != '':
                allWords.append(word)

    file.close()


def readFileToConvertWordsToIntegers(dictionary, fileName, allDocuments, allLabels, label):

    file = codecs.open(fileName, encoding='utf-8')
    document = []
    for line in file:
        line = line.lower().encode('utf-8')
        words = line.split()
        for word in words:
            word = word.translate(None, string.punctuation)
            if word in dictionary:
                index = dictionary[word]
            else:
                index = 0  # dictionary['UNK'] 
            document.append(index)
        allDocuments.append(document)
        allLabels.append(label)

    file.close()


vocabulary_size = 10000

def build_dataset(words):
  count = [['UNK', -1]]
  count.extend(collections.Counter(words).most_common(vocabulary_size - 1))
  dictionary = dict()
  for word, _ in count:
    dictionary[word] = len(dictionary)
  data = list()
  unk_count = 0
  for word in words:
    if word in dictionary:
      index = dictionary[word]
    else:
      index = 0  # dictionary['UNK']
      unk_count = unk_count + 1
    data.append(index)
  count[0][1] = unk_count
  reverse_dictionary = dict(zip(dictionary.values(), dictionary.keys())) 
  return dictionary, reverse_dictionary



fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/neg/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFile(file, allWords)

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/test/train/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFile(file, allWords)

print(len(allWords))

dictionary, reverse_dictionary = build_dataset(allWords)
del allWords  # Hint to reduce memory.

print(len(dictionary))

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/neg/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFileToConvertWordsToIntegers(dictionary, file, allDocuments, allLabels, 0)

fileList = glob.glob("/Users/inanc/Desktop/aclImdb/train/pos/*.txt")
for file in fileList:
    readFileToConvertWordsToIntegers(dictionary, file, allDocuments, allLabels, 1)

print(len(allDocuments))
print(len(allLabels))

c = list(zip(allDocuments, allLabels)) # shuffle them partitioning

random.shuffle(c)

allDocuments, allLabels = zip(*c)

trainX = allDocuments[:22500]
testX = allDocuments[22500:]

trainY = allLabels[:22500]
testY = allLabels[22500:]


#counter=collections.Counter(trainY)
#print(counter)


trainY = to_categorical(trainY, nb_classes=2)
testY = to_categorical(testY, nb_classes=2)

trainX = pad_sequences(trainX, maxlen=100, value=0.)
testX = pad_sequences(testX, maxlen=100, value=0.)
# Converting labels to binary vectors
trainY = to_categorical(trainY, nb_classes=2)
testY = to_categorical(testY, nb_classes=2)

# Network building
net = tflearn.input_data([None, 100])
net = tflearn.embedding(net, input_dim=vocabulary_size, output_dim=128)
net = tflearn.lstm(net, 128, dropout=0.8)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 2, activation='softmax')
net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.001,
                         loss='categorical_crossentropy')

# Training
model = tflearn.DNN(net, tensorboard_verbose=0)
model.fit(trainX, trainY, validation_set=(testX, testY), show_metric=True,
          batch_size=32)
predictions = model.predict(trainX)
print(predictions)

issue_title: Add checkout form preauth initialize
Redis hook interface should exactly match StrictRedis
issue_body:
The StrictRedis client's [interface](https://github.com/andymccurdy/redis-py/blob/master/redis/client.py#L1130) for `set` is:
`set(self, name, value, ex=None, px=None, nx=False, xx=False)`

We should match that in the client hook to support the case when clients don't specify the keyword argument. There was a nasty bug in production since we was invoking the Redis client like so:
`set('foo', 'bar', 10)` instead of `set('foo', 'bar', ex=10)`. While it may be good practice to always specify the kwarg, we should still support the ability to only use positional args.

issue_title: Accessibility of examples for interactive icons
issue_body: According to the post on how to use icons accessibly ( http://fontawesome.io/accessibility/) for icons that convey interaction and therefore should be consumed by a screen reader, the suggested example makes use of an anchor tag with a title attribute that wraps the icon that is set to aria-hidden. The use of a title attribute on an anchor element is considered to be a challenge for many user agents and should be used with caution (as per http://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/H33.html). In this instance a more accessible example maybe to include a span within the anchor tag that is accessible only to a screen reader with content to provide link purpose (as is used in other examples on the font awesome site) or if this is not feasible to use the aria-label attribute rather than the title attribute as a means of proving link definition to screen reader users.

issue_title: Rename input to step_input?
issue_body:
It occurs to me that we should rename our `input` to `sos_input`, `output` to `sos_output`, and `depends` to `sos_depends`. `_input` etc can be kept unchanged.

The reason is simple, `input`, `output` are too common. `input` is a built-in function and `output` is a common name, so it is very likely they will be used by users, and cause surprises. At least for now,

```
input('a')
```
in a SoS kernel and `python3` kernel will behave differently,

This will be a big change and will force us to change many documentations and re-record multiple videos, not sure if there can be a walkaround for it.

issue_title: Inconsistent inherited docstrings
issue_body:
A few estimators subclass estimators from other ML packages (e.g. scikit-learn) and when a method is not overwritten, the docstring from the parent package is used directly in the documentation, which is not necessarily consistent. For instance,
 * docstrings inherited from scikit-learn where [the variable dtype includes sparse arrays](http://dask-ml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/search.html?q=sparse&check_keywords=yes&area=default)
 * ["see also" sections](http://dask-ml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modules/generated/dask_ml.linear_model.PartialPerceptron.html?highlight=sparse) that points to scikit-learn estimators etc.

I wonder if some automatic re-writing of the non-overwritten methods could partially address this...

issue_title: How to get rid of bad depths below min-range
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415, D435 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 5.9.14 | 
| Operating System & Version |  Win10 | 
| SDK Version                            |  2.14.0 | 
| Segment			|  3D Scanning | 

We use the D415 for 3D Scanning and we frequently run into the issue of users getting too close (below min-range) with the camera, causing a lot of bad/stray points in the depth image, causing some corruption in the resulting model.

We were trying the D4 presets and the tuning parameters but unfortunately they do have a lot of side effects so, although we can theoretically find a setting that minimizes bad depths below min-range, it's not practical since we'd also be getting rid of most of the good depth values.

Hence, we're looking for a way to filter bad points from the depth image before they can corrupt the model. Is there some sort of confidence map that we could get through libRS to help with the filtering? Any other post-processing setting?

Btw, we appreciate all the existing post-processing options but in terms of importance, filtering bad depths below min-range and bad depths due to over-exposure (https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues/1912) would be **way** above the offered downsampling and spatial/temporal filtering.

issue_title: C++ hardware reset (not ROS)
issue_body:
* Before opening a new issue, we wanted to provide you with some useful suggestions (Click "Preview" above for a better view):

    * Consider checking out SDK [examples](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/examples#sample-code-for-intel-realsense-cameras).
    * Have you looked in our [documentations](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/doc#useful-links)?
    * Is you question a [frequently asked one](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/wiki/Troubleshooting-Q%26A)?
    * Try [searching our GitHub Issues](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) (open and closed) for a similar issue.

* All users are welcomed to report bugs, ask questions, suggest or request enhancements and generally feel free to open new issue, even if they haven't followed any of the suggestions above :)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | { D400 } | 
| Firmware Version                   | (V2.13) | 
| Operating System & Version |   { Linux (Ubuntu 16)  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  16.04.1                                      | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |  { 2.14.1 }                          | 
| Language                            |  {C++ with OpenCV }                          | 
| Segment			|                          | 

### Issue Description
I'm having a similar issue to some others where when I plug my camera in once, it works fine, but if I stop the stream and restart it I get a "No Frames Received error" (on the Realsense-Viewer. It just hangs on all the examples / my own script). The only workaround I've figured is to unplug and replug the camera back in, and then it works just fine again... once. It consistently only works once.

I know how to manual reset it using the Realsense-Viewer, but I can't figure out how to code wise. (My workaround currently is to just hardware reset the cameras at the beginning of my script.)

If I try:
> rs2::device dev = device_you_are_using();
> dev.hardware_reset(); //Will disconnect the camera and reconnect it.

I get a "'device_you_are_using' was not declared in scope" error and it fails to compile.

Similarly, if I try: 
> rs2::context ctx;
> rs2::device dev = get_device_from_context(ctx);
> dev.hardware_reset();
> rs2::device_hub hub(ctx);
> dev = hub.wait_for_device(); //Note that device hub will get any device, if you have more than 1 connected it could return the other device

I get a "'get_device_from_context' was not declared in scope" error and it also fails.

Could someone please explain how I can implement this in C++? There is very little / no documentation on hardware_reset(). Thank you!

issue_title: [proposal] Add support for throttling per route
issue_body:
API Gateway has various forms of API throttling.  This proposal adds support for throttling at the routing level.  This allows you to specify a rate and burst limit per `@app.route`.

Similar to CORS and authorizers, there will be a new param, `throttle`, that takes an instance of `ThrottleConfig`:

```python

@app.route('/', throttle=ThrottleConfig(rate=10, burst=15))
def index(): pass
```

`ThrottleConfig` accepts two arguments, `rate` and `burst`:

```python
class ThrottleConfig(object):
    def __init__(self, rate: int, burst: int): pass
```

The `rate` and `burst` params map to the corresponding concepts described in the [API Gateway throttling docs](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-request-throttling.html)

issue_title: Proposal: search for local config-files/readers automatically
issue_body:
EUMETSAT has many Level 0, 1, and 2 products in different internal formats. These products are only used in-house and are not send out to the users. Therefore, `satpy`-readers for such products would quickly pollute `satpy/etc/readers/`-directory with `*.yaml`-files that are only useful for a small amount of PyTroll users.

The workaround with current implementation is to use `ppp_config_dir`-keyword when creating a `Scene`-object:
```
scn = Scene(fname, reader='my_reader', ppp_config_dir='/local/config/path')
```
This is a proposal for a possibility to add a local config directory where config files would be automatically searched. Proposed solution by modifying `config_search_paths`-method in `satpy/config.py` by setting an environmental variable, e.g. `SATPY_LOCAL_CONFIG`

#### Proposed modification

```python
def config_search_paths(filename, *search_dirs, **kwargs):
    # Get the environment variable value every time (could be set dynamically)
    # FIXME: Consider removing the 'magic' environment variable all together
    CONFIG_PATH = get_environ_config_dir()
    LOCAL_PATH = os.environ.get('SATPY_LOCAL_CONFIG')
    paths = [filename, os.path.basename(filename)]
    paths += [os.path.join(search_dir, filename) for search_dir in search_dirs]
    if LOCAL_PATH:
        paths += [os.path.join(LOCAL_PATH, filename)]
    # FUTURE: Remove CONFIG_PATH because it should be included as a search_dir
    paths += [os.path.join(CONFIG_PATH, filename),
              os.path.join(PACKAGE_CONFIG_PATH, filename)]

    if kwargs.get("check_exists", True):
        paths = [x for x in paths if os.path.isfile(x)]

    # flip the order of the list so builtins are loaded first
    return paths[::-1]
```

Any ideas, comments, thoughts? Would such modification be useful for other institutes/organizations? Or is this just silly? ;)

issue_title: restoration.unwrap_phase could take dtype into account for default wrapping period
issue_body:
Currently, `restoration.unwrap_phase` uncondtionally casts its data to double and uses a period of `[-pi, pi]`.  I'd argue that when the input type is an integer type, this is probably never the expected behavior; especially for "small" integer types ((u)int{8,16}), the "correct" period is probably just the span of the dtype (so 0..255 for uint8, etc).  So I'd suggest
- if the input dtype is one of uint8, int8, uint16, int16: use the span of the dtype as period, return an image of the "next" dtype (so uint8->uint16, etc.) (this can overflow if there are more than 256 wraps in an image but I expect this to be unlikely in practice?  also this can always be worked around by switching to the second point)
- if the input dtype is a 32 or 64 bit integer, or floating, cast to double and proceed as before (one could argue that (u)int32 should fall under the first point, I don't really care either way).
  The alternative would be to provide a "period" kwarg to, well, indicate the period, but we'd still need dtype upcast rules anyways and I think these are sensible defaults (and the previous behavior can always be recovered with an explicit cast to double).

issue_title: Rename netting channel `contract_balance` property
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The netting channel has a property called at the moment `contract_balance` as can be seen [here](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/c5bec6d4daa41c52fb68bca20cd96f1b4942d122/raiden/channel/netting_channel.py#L214).

The name is confusing because the contract balance of a netting channel is understood as the balance of the entire contract in token and not just one participant's side.

## Solution

Rename the attribute and all the places it is used to `total_deposit` as that is what the attribute represents. It is an always increasing number signifying how much our side (as a participant in the channel) has deposited in the contract so far.


## Tasklist

- [ ] Rename `contract_balance` to `total_deposit` in the netting channel

issue_title: Create Image instance from id
issue_body:
It would be useful to be able to create an instance of `DockerImage` from its `id` without name or tag.

Any ideas how to edit parameters to not break the backward compatibility?

```
class DockerImage(Image):
    def __init__(self, repository, tag="latest", pull_policy=DockerImagePullPolicy.IF_NOT_PRESENT):
```
> Usecase: Loading image class from colin, when the user set the image-id isntead of the name.

Some questions:

- What will be the `get_full_name` of the image created from the id?
    - None
    - id
    - Try to find one from metadata?
- Try same approach for containers?
- When we are in this, we can provide better work with image names in general. What about something like [in atomic_reactor](https://github.com/projectatomic/atomic-reactor/blob/master/atomic_reactor/util.py#L57)?

issue_title: Misfeature with non-installed ipython
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...**
  - [X] bug report
  - [ ] feature request


## Current Behavior

In an environment that does not have `ipython` installed, the base version of `ipython` is still available.  However, this runs in the base environment, and does not see packages specific to the local environment.  This has bitten me **many** times by wasting time figuring out why an import isn't working (or why the package doesn't match the API of the version in the current environment, but rather of the version in the base environment).

### Steps to Reproduce

```
% conda create -n no-ipython humidity -c davidmertz -y  # Silly example package
[... creating ...]
% conda activate no-ipython
(no-ipython) % ipython
Python 3.6.4 |Anaconda custom (64-bit)| (default, Jan 16 2018, 12:04:33)
Type 'copyright', 'credits' or 'license' for more information
IPython 6.1.0 -- An enhanced Interactive Python. Type '?' for help.
In [1]: import humidity
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ModuleNotFoundError                       Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-1-5cdf29aced62> in <module>()
----> 1 import humidity
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'humidity'
In [2]:
Do you really want to exit ([y]/n)? y
(no-ipython) % which ipython
/Users/dmertz/anaconda3/bin/ipython
(no-ipython) % conda install ipython -y
[... installing ...]
(no-ipython) % which ipython
/Users/dmertz/anaconda3/envs/no-ipython/bin/ipython
(no-ipython) % ipython
Python 3.6.4 |Anaconda, Inc.| (default, Jan 16 2018, 12:04:33)
Type 'copyright', 'credits' or 'license' for more information
IPython 6.2.1 -- An enhanced Interactive Python. Type '?' for help.
In [1]: import humidity
In [2]:
```


## Expected Behavior

```
(no-ipython) % ipython  # When ipython is not installed in the environment
-bash: ipython: command not found
```

## Environment Information

As created in sample code... or any other environment without ipython installed.

issue_title: conda handling of invalid auth tokens
issue_body:
Using v4.3.18 `conda update conda` fails due to borked auth token that, on first inspection, doesn't even appear to exist to a user (i.e. it looks like they are logged out).  conda DOES tell me what to do to solve the problem (which is good) -- execute `anaconda logout` --  but I feel like the problem shouldn't have happened in the first place.

When I try to do simple operations `conda` fails with an HTTP 401 error message about invalid auth token for accessing the `anaconda-fusion` channel, even though the channel is public/world-readable (I thought there was an issue out for that already, but I can't find it), and worse when I go and do `anaconda whoami` it appears that I am logged out as it reports `Anonymous User`.  Because of the other conda bug of having excessive DEBUG and INFO output appearing (even when I haven't asked for it) I can now see that `anaconda-client` has noticed the expired/borked auth token, but that is only mentioned in the DEBUG output.

Expected behavior:

1. `anaconda whoami` reports `Anonymous User` and should only do so if there is no auth token.
2. `conda` needs to notice if it has a borked auth token and not feed garbage to the repository.
3. Anaconda Repository should not care about auth tokens for read-operations if the channel is world-readable

```bash
0097-ijstokes:~ ijstokes$ conda update conda
Fetching package metadata ...
CondaHTTPError: HTTP 401 UNAUTHORIZED for url <https://conda.anaconda.org/anaconda-fusion/osx-64/repodata.json>
Elapsed: 00:00.427186
CF-RAY: 36180786de692a91-SEA

The remote server has indicated you are using invalid credentials for this channel.

If the remote site is anaconda.org or follows the Anaconda Server API, you
will need to
  (a) remove the invalid token from your system with `anaconda logout`, optionally
      followed by collecting a new token with `anaconda login`, or
  (b) provide conda with a valid token directly.

Further configuration help can be found at <https://conda.io/docs/config.html>.


0097-ijstokes:~ ijstokes$ anaconda whoami
Using Anaconda API: https://api.anaconda.org

issue_title: ORDER BY query quantifies given order by string (which might be an alias) with a table name
issue_body:
**Example**
ORDER BY query using the alias without a join:
```
str(Query.from_(a).select(a.b, fn.Sum(a.c).as_('alias')).groupby(a.c).orderby('alias') )
=> 'SELECT "b",SUM("c") "alias" FROM "a" GROUP BY "c" ORDER BY "alias"'
```
ORDER BY query using the alias with a join:
```
str(Query.from_(a).join(b).on(a.b_id == b.id).select(a.foo, fn.Sum(a.int_column).as_('alias')).groupby(a.foo).orderby('alias') )
=> 'SELECT "a"."foo",SUM("a"."int_column") "alias" FROM "a" JOIN "b" ON "a"."b_id"="b"."id" GROUP BY "a"."foo" ORDER BY "a"."alias"'
```

**Issue**
The second example shows that when using a string in the `orderby` which might be an alias, is quantified with the FROM-table.

**Suggestion**
Don't quantify with the FROM-table in case of a string.
I think it's the coders responsibility to properly quantify, if necessary. The databases would complain for invalid SQL in case of ambiguous column names.

Let me know if there's a reason to quantify it that I don't see.

issue_title: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: org.tensorframes.impl.DebugRowOps
issue_body:
I build the jar by follow the readme,
and then run it in pycharm 
https://www.dropbox.com/s/qmrs72l0p8p4bc2/Screen%20Shot%202016-07-06%20at%2011.40.26%20PM.png?dl=0
I add the self build jar as content root, I guess that's cause the error, 

line 11 is     x = tfs.block(df, "x")

code:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import tensorframes as tfs
from pyspark.shell import sqlContext
from pyspark.sql import Row

data = [Row(x=float(x)) for x in range(10)]
df = sqlContext.createDataFrame(data)

with tf.Graph().as_default() as g:
    # The TensorFlow placeholder that corresponds to column 'x'.
    # The shape of the placeholder is automatically inferred from the DataFrame.
    x = tfs.block(df, "x")
    # The output that adds 3 to x
    z = tf.add(x, 3, name='z')
    # The resulting dataframe
    df2 = tfs.map_blocks(z, df)

# The transform is lazy as for most DataFrame operations. This will trigger it:
df2.collect()
```

issue_title: Multiple scorers
issue_body:
Newest SKLearn docs(http://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.GridSearchCV.html) say that:

> scoring: For evaluating multiple metrics, either give a list of (unique) strings or a dict with names as keys and callables as values.

However, `spark-sklearn` does not seem to accept `scoring` parameter in a form of list.

Is there any easy way to accomplish that(submitting few scorers, eg. `scoring = ['accuracy', 'f1', 'roc_auc', 'average_precision']`? Or is it perhaps in your roadmap? If not, how much time(approximately) would the implementation take for somebody new to the project(like me)?

issue_title: Potential circular import in types
issue_body:
Hi,

I've been trying to import the DateTime type, but I'm getting occasional import errors that seem to point towards a circular import somewhere in the graphene types. I'd love to send a pull-request, but I'm still not sure where the circular import might be exactly. Here's how to reproduce it and how I'm working around it currently:

Problem:
```python
>>> import graphene
>>> graphene.types.datetime
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: module 'graphene.types' has no attribute 'datetime'
```

Workaround:
```python
>>> from graphene.types import datetime
>>> import graphene
>>> graphene.types.datetime
<module 'graphene.types.datetime' from '/.../graphene/types/datetime.py'>
```

If you've got any pointers I'll be happy to try to fix and send a PR!

issue_title: Fields with numbers in names do not capitalize correctly
issue_body:
I noticed that the field with names containing numbers (e.g. `field_i18n`) doesn't capitalize correctly.

For example:
`correct_field` becomes `correctField`, but `field_i18n` becomes `field_I18N` and `t1e2s3t` becomes `t1E2S3T` which is obvious incorrect.

This caused by using `.title` method in `str_converter.py` instead of `.capitalize`. Title method is suitable for use with words, but field names are single word.

issue_title: Handle connections in 2.0
issue_body:
I upgrading to 2.0 of graphene and related packages.

Without any changes I get
`Exception: ConnectionField's now need a explicit ConnectionType for Nodes.`

So I added the connection explicitly like the upgrade guide say `DepartureConnection`
```python
class DepartureSchema(SQLAlchemyObjectType):
    """
    A departure
    """
    class Meta:
        model = Departure
        interfaces = (graphene.Node, relay.Node)


class DepartureConnection(graphene.Connection):
    class Meta:
        node = DepartureSchema


class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    departures = relay.ConnectionField(DepartureConnection)
```

But now I get 
```
graphql.error.located_error.GraphQLLocatedError: Resolved value from the connection field have to be iterable or instance of DepartureConnection. Received "None"
```

So I'm probably missing something. I think the documentation/example with relay needs to be adjusted for 2.0. If I just knew what is needed in 2.0 I could do a PR

Thanks for these great libraries!

issue_title: Test streams are uselessly verbose
issue_body:
I have an issue with testrepository, where test repositories are very big (around the 25M+). I looked at the content, and the vast majority is made of stuff like this: 

Content-Type: text/plain;charset=utf8
pythonlogging:'something'
0

My test suite used a lot of LoggerFixture, and I believe that's the cause of it. But it seems I get an event even if the logger doesn't get anything, which seems like a waste. We shouldn't add the test details if there isn't any data to log.

I'm opening the issue in testtools because I think it's where I can fix it.

issue_title: Confusing installation failures with PyPy 2.5.0 or Python 2.7.8
issue_body:
Trying to install ZEO 5.0 with the PyPy that's on Travis currently fails in a [very confusing way](https://travis-ci.org/zodb/relstorage/jobs/158471721#L261):
```
7.38s$ pip install -U -e ".[test]"
Obtaining file:///home/travis/build/zodb/relstorage
Collecting perfmetrics (from RelStorage==2.0.0b7.dev0)
/home/travis/virtualenv/pypy-2.5.0/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py:318: SNIMissingWarning: An HTTPS request has been made, but the SNI (Subject Name Indication) extension to TLS is not available on this platform. This may cause the server to present an incorrect TLS certificate, which can cause validation failures. You can upgrade to a newer version of Python to solve this. For more information, see https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/security.html#snimissingwarning.
  SNIMissingWarning
/home/travis/virtualenv/pypy-2.5.0/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py:122: InsecurePlatformWarning: A true SSLContext object is not available. This prevents urllib3 from configuring SSL appropriately and may cause certain SSL connections to fail. You can upgrade to a newer version of Python to solve this. For more information, see https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/security.html#insecureplatformwarning.
  InsecurePlatformWarning
  Downloading perfmetrics-2.0.tar.gz
Collecting ZODB>=4.4.2 (from RelStorage==2.0.0b7.dev0)
  Downloading ZODB-5.0.0.tar.gz (467kB)
    100% |████████████████████████████████| 471kB 2.2MB/s 
Collecting ZEO>=4.2.0 (from RelStorage==2.0.0b7.dev0)
  Downloading ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz (328kB)
    100% |████████████████████████████████| 337kB 3.0MB/s 
No files/directories in /tmp/pip-build-KcLvJI/ZEO/pip-egg-info (from PKG-INFO)
```

Locally I tracked it down to this command failing:
```
$ python setup.py egg_info --egg-base pip-egg-info
This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher
```

(PyPy 2.5.0 claims to be Python 2.7.8.)

pip naturally buries this output by default. What's worse, though is that, when the script exits like that, it exits with a successful exit code:
```
$ python setup.py egg_info --egg-base pip-egg-info
This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher
$ echo $?
0
```

(I'm guessing that allowing Python 2.7.8 is not going to happen because SSL. So I'll have to pin versions back just on Travis, just for PyPy.)

However, if we can make the exit code a failure code then pip will at least bail out early with a reasonable error message (I edited the script to sys.exit(1) instead of 0):
```
$ pip install  ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz
Processing ./ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher

    ----------------------------------------
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /var/folders/y5/x7pvzk651c3dqkllbxd1jd280000gn/T/pip-uraGDb-build/
```

issue_title: replace generic exceptions with galsim exceptions
issue_body:
There are many errors that should be recoverable but galsim throws a generic
error.  This is a problem because it may not be clear from where this error
originates.  One cannot distinguish the recoverable from non-recoverable
errors.

For example, a pipeline may run many calls to galsim methods; tens is not
unusual. Each of these may throw a `RuntimeError` or `ValueError`.   The
pipeline, and galsim itself, will intersperse other python function calls with
galsim calls, many of which may throw the same errors.

The difference is that most `RuntimeError` or `ValueError` thrown by the python
standard library are not recoverable, they signify a bug in the program, and
the program should crash and the user should fix the code.

But many times when galsim throws such an error it is not a bug, galsim simply
does not know what to do with the inputs or has some internal numerical
restrictions that are not met.  Often one can just re-call the method with
modified parameters, or otherwise deal with this an move on.  If I *knew* the
exception was a galsim exception I could write some robust code to deal with
the situation.

An example came up today when the fft code threw a `RuntimeError` because the
sheared image would require too large an FFT.  This is not a bug, I can deal
with this quite cleanly at run time, but I can't tell if this `RuntimeError`
is coming from galsim or not.

I propose replacing some uses of `RuntimeError` or `ValueError` with something
like `GalsimValueError`, `GalsimRangeError` or whatever, inheriting from a bas
`GalsimError` class.    These can then be caught and dealt with robustly.

issue_title: specify record contents accepted by storage
issue_body:
An inconsistency between memory and PG storage led to shipping broken `accounts` plugin (#1224).

It was caused by memory storage not touching the contents of given record (contained a bytes value), whereas PG storage converted it to str. See discussion in #1227.

Memory can be made like PG storage with a moderate cost (serialiaze/deserialize records with ujson), like in PR #1237. This leaves potential errors in production when somebody sends non utf-8 encoded input where we only tested with utf-8 input.

But, as @glasserc pointed out, storage could also be made stricter on datatypes it accepts in records (i.e. REJECT bytes). Then errors will happen during development, which is better.

To do this, I think one would have to scan records beforehand or add an option to ujson to do it or retuirn to standard package json.

issue_body:

Fedora 25

0.1.4

urllib3==1.22
wcwidth==0.1.7
z3-solver==4.5.1.0.post2


### Summary of the problem

If `state.new_symbolic_buffer(x)` is called and `x` is a long instead of an int then an assertion in `expression.Array` fails. 

### Step to reproduce the behavior

Utilise `state.invoke_model` to trigger a model of a function that takes a numeric argument. The argument reaches the model as a long, not an int. If the model then uses this argument in a call to `new_symbolic_buffer` the assertion failure occurs.

```
# Hook for function with the following signature 
# size_t read_data(const char *fname, char *dest, size_t count) 
def read_data(state, fname, dest, count):
    print "Introducing %d symbolic bytes at 0x%x" % (count, dest)
    buf = state.new_symbolic_buffer(count)
    state.cpu.write_bytes(dest, buf)
    with m.locked_context() as ctx:
        ctx['input_addr'] = dest

    return count

@m.hook(READ_DATA)
def read_data_model(state):
    print "Invoking model for read_data"
    state.invoke_model(read_data)
```

### Expected behavior

Either the model invocation should provide the arguments to the model as an int, or the expression creation code should not be fussy about whether it receives an int or a long. I wasn't sure which is more desirable in terms of manticore's design, hence no pull request =)

issue_title: Add an option to filter fasta by line number
issue_body:
Make it so `filter-fasta.py` can be given a file name of (sorted) line numbers and then only print those lines to output. This will be useful for fast(er) filtering of JSON and FASTA input to `noninteractive-alignment-panel.py`. That fast filtering would then be done in the following steps:

```sh
$ bzcat files*.json.bz2 | grep -n pattern > temp
$ cut -f1 -d: < temp > line-numbers
$ cut -f2- -d: | bzip2 > new.json.bz2
$ filter-fasta.py --printLines line-numbers > new.fasta
```

(The two `cut` calls could be handled by one program too if we want).

The idea here is that we can grep for some things (like a subject title) quickly in the `json.bz2` files without having to parse the JSON on each line (or even use Python at all). Then we get the line numbers and pull the corresponding sequences out of the FASTA (`noninteractive-alignment-panel.py` needs both the JSON and the FASTA).

The reason for providing line numbers in a file is that if we have a billion line numbers we want, the shell will break if they're all on the command line. Reading them from a file is no problem.

issue_title: config_hook does not get specified arguments
issue_body:
I would like to adapt the config of an experiment with a config_hook

The setup is like this:

    ex = Experiment() 

    def hook(config, command_name, logger):
        config.update({'hook': True})
        return config
    
    @ex.config_hook(hook)
    @ex.automain
    def main(hook, other_config):
        do_stuff()

The tests for the arguments of the hook work well, i.e. sacred raises an error if the hook would take only 1 argument. However, executing this script returns:

    TypeError: hook() takes exactly 3 arguments (1 given)

Unfortunately, there is not much documentation for the config_hooks, and neither for the pre_run_hook. I also could not find anywhere the code which executes the config_hooks (a search for `config_hook` only finds ingredient.py with the definition, no execution.
Is there any possibility of adapting the config directly before running the main?
I prefer selecting config-files with the commadn line, but based on this config-file I would like to make adaptions to the configuration of the run.

issue_title: PasswordResetView proposal to move the User.is_active check from database to Python
AssertionError at RootView
issue_body:
This is because RootView doesn't have `permission_classes` declared, and `rest_framework.permissions.DjangoModelPermissionsOrAnonReadOnly` is my default permissions class in `settings.REST_FRAMEWORK['DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES']`.

Adding the following to RootView solves it:
```
    permission_classes = (
        permissions.AllowAny,
    )
```

Perhaps related to #31

issue_title: Versioning changes
issue_body:
The model specification for a widget (i.e., the list of model traits) is implemented at least two places: the kernel and the frontend. Because we want different frontend and kernel-side implementations to exist, the model state specification should probably not be tied to any particular package name or version. Also, I think the view should be defined by the model version it supports and the name/module. (i.e., the version should essentially be '*'). In order for that to happen, we probably need to get rid of custom messages (which are sent from the view to the kernel) and instead route all of that information through the state.

The model version should only support the major part of the version number (i.e., essentially be an integer). Any change in the actual attributes of the models (whether adding, deleting, or even changing intent of the attribute) should be a breaking change in the model specification.

CC @SylvainCorlay

issue_body:
In function `usb_lsusb_string` in the file `list_ports_posix.py` there is an undefined symbol at line 80.

Here's the code:

    def usb_lsusb_string(sysfs_path):
        bus, dev = os.path.basename(os.path.realpath(sysfs_path)).split('-')
        try:
            desc = popen(['lsusb', '-v', '-s', '%s:%s' % (bus, dev)])
            # descriptions from device
            iManufacturer = re_group('iManufacturer\s+\w+ (.+)', desc)
            iProduct = re_group('iProduct\s+\w+ (.+)', desc)
            iSerial = re_group('iSerial\s+\w+ (.+)', desc) or ''
            # descriptions from kernel
            idVendor = re_group('idVendor\s+0x\w+ (.+)', desc)
            idProduct = re_group('idProduct\s+0x\w+ (.+)', desc)
            # create descriptions. prefer text from device, fall back to the others
            return '%s %s %s' % (iManufacturer or idVendor, iProduct or idProduct, iSerial)
        except IOError:
            return base

If an IOError occurs when trying to use popen, the function tries to return `base` which is undefined.
Occurs on pyserial 2.6, Ubuntu 15.10 x64.

issue_title: Handler's rep is executed twice during encoding
issue_body:
One execution happens during `marshal` and another during `emit_encoded`. I think it is possible to reuse representation between the two. Test case:
```
import transit.writer
import StringIO

class rep(object):

    @staticmethod
    def tag(v):
        return 'tag'

    @staticmethod
    def rep(v):
        print 'rep'
        return []

class A(object):
    pass

w = transit.writer.Writer(StringIO.StringIO(), 'json')
w.register(A, rep)
w.write({'a': A()})
```
outputs:
```
rep
rep
```

issue_title: Magic money querying ability does not chain
issue_body:
Take, for example, `VanillaMoneyFieldTestCase.testCurrencySearch`. In the line

    shouldBeEmpty = ModelWithVanillaMoneyField.objects.filter(money__lt=otherMoney)

if you replace `objects` with `objects.all().all()`, then the test fails.

Basically, you end up with a vanilla `QuerySet` that does not 'understand money'. However, there is no warning - the query appears to succeed, but returns the wrong answer. This hugely violates expectations - people normally chain ORM methods a lot, and don't expect them to suddenly behave differently because of an earlier `filter` or `order_by` etc.

This is a serious flaw in this feature, IMO. If it can't be fixed, I'd recommend pulling it completely. The alternative method (explicit filtering against the `_currency` field) will always work.

To solve it, I think you need a custom `QuerySet` subclass. It is this that should have `understands_money` applied. And you then need to do patching that will return this `QuerySet` rather than anything else. I may be able to work something up for this.

issue_title: Cannot SELECT FROM JOIN
issue_body:
How can I get the result from two tables' JOIN? I've prepared a sample to show that even when I specify "$db" the query is still invalid:
``` python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from __future__ import unicode_literals, print_function

import json
import logging

from infi.clickhouse_orm import database, engines, fields, models

logging.getLogger("requests.packages.urllib3").setLevel(logging.WARNING)
LOGGER = logging.getLogger()
logging.basicConfig(
        format="%(levelname)s  -> %(message)s", level=logging.DEBUG)


class Foo(models.Model):
    engine = engines.Memory()
    id = fields.UInt8Field()


class Bar(Foo):
    b = fields.UInt8Field()


def print_res(query):
    LOGGER.debug(query)
    try:
        print(json.dumps([row.to_dict() for row in DB.select(query)]))
    except database.DatabaseException as e:
        LOGGER.error(e)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    DB = database.Database(db_name='test')
    DB.drop_table(Foo)
    DB.drop_table(Bar)
    DB.create_table(Foo)
    DB.create_table(Bar)
    N = 3

    DB.insert([Foo(id=i) for i in range(3)])
    DB.insert([Bar(id=i, b=i * i) for i in range(3)])
    LOGGER.info("Without $db specifier")
    print_res("SELECT * FROM {}".format(Foo.table_name()))
    print_res("SELECT * FROM {}".format(Bar.table_name()))
    print_res(
        "SELECT b FROM {} ALL LEFT JOIN {} USING id".format(Foo.table_name(), Bar.table_name()))

    LOGGER.info("With $db specifier")
    print_res("SELECT * FROM $db.{}".format(Foo.table_name()))
    print_res("SELECT * FROM $db.{}".format(Bar.table_name()))
    print_res(
        "SELECT b FROM $db.{} ALL LEFT JOIN $db.{} USING id".format(Foo.table_name(), Bar.table_name()))
```

outputs this

```
INFO  -> Without $db specifier
DEBUG  -> SELECT * FROM foo
ERROR  -> Code: 60, e.displayText() = DB::Exception: Table default.foo doesn't exist., e.what() = DB::Exception

DEBUG  -> SELECT * FROM bar
ERROR  -> Code: 60, e.displayText() = DB::Exception: Table default.bar doesn't exist., e.what() = DB::Exception

DEBUG  -> SELECT b FROM foo ALL LEFT JOIN bar USING id
ERROR  -> Code: 60, e.displayText() = DB::Exception: Table default.foo doesn't exist., e.what() = DB::Exception

INFO  -> With $db specifier
DEBUG  -> SELECT * FROM $db.foo
[{"id": 0}, {"id": 1}, {"id": 2}]
DEBUG  -> SELECT * FROM $db.bar
[{"b": 0, "id": 0}, {"b": 1, "id": 1}, {"b": 4, "id": 2}]
DEBUG  -> SELECT b FROM $db.foo ALL LEFT JOIN $db.bar USING id
ERROR  -> Code: 60, e.displayText() = DB::Exception: Table default.test.bar doesn't exist., e.what() = DB::Exception
```

However, the JOINed query should output result like that:
```
$ clickhouse-client --database=test --query="SELECT b FROM foo ALL LEFT JOIN bar USING id"

issue_title: Looser requirements in setup.py
issue_body:
Hi, am starting to use PyFilesystem and S3FS and I like it. One issue I'm having however is that some of my applications require older versions of boto3 (1.4.0) and botocore (1.4.28). 
I've done some tests and it doesn't seem to be an issue with `fs_s3fs`, so I was wondering if you could relax the requirements rules and use for example
```
REQUIREMENTS = [
    "boto3>=1.4.0, <1.5",
    "fs>=2.0",
    "six>=1.10"
]
```

This is also more in line with https://blog.miguelgrinberg.com/post/the-package-dependency-blues 
Happy to submit a PR if that'd help.

issue_title: open_fs(writeable=True) doesn't care about read-only filesystems
issue_body:
Just noticed this quite important bug: when calling `fs.open_fs` with `writeable` set to `True` (which according to the doc requires the given FS or FS URL to be writeable), nothing happens even if the given FS or FS URL maps to a read-only filesystem. I tried rolling back to `2.0.2`, issue was also there.

Can be reproduced with
```python
>>> fs.open_fs("/path/to/an/existing/file.zip")
```

I think the registry should raise an error (`CreateFailed`, `ResourceReadOnly` ?) if the returned filesystem is read-only according to its metadata, but for now it relies on the individual openers doing their things (and since none of them actually care about the `writeable` keyword, it doesn't do anything)

issue_body:

issue_title: Missing a few CodeCommit & CloudFormation actions
issue_body:
I was going recreating [this AWS policy example](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codepipeline/latest/userguide/iam-identity-based-access-control.html#view-default-service-role-policy) with Troposphere and awacs and I found that awacs was missing a couple actions.

issue_title: Bug:  stack names should not be lowercased
issue_body:
This sounds a bit nit picky but we ran into this today when trying to deploy a stack.   The problem is that we are deploying to an account where we're forced to use a cloudformation service role. 

https://stacker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/config.html?highlight=service_role#service-role

The service role forces us to use certain resource prefixes, and unfortunately we currently have a policy that restricts stack names using camel case of `StcEcs` for our project team.

This is our setup (snippets of..)

conf.env

`namespace: StcEcs`

stacker.yaml

```
stacks:
  - name: teamcityAgentWindows
```

Actual Log Output

```
[2018-07-10T14:04:38] Using interactive AWS provider mode.
[2018-07-10T14:04:38] Tailing stack: stcecs-teamcityAgentWindows
~
[2018-07-10T14:04:39] An error occurred (AccessDenied) when calling the CreateStack operation: User: arn:aws:sts::<obfuscated_account>:assumed-role/ADFS-OpsSupport-StcEcs/<obfuscated.email> is not authorized to perform: cloudformation:CreateStack on resource: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-west-2:<obfuscated_account>:stack/stcecs-teamcityAgentWindows/*
```


Expected log Output

`Tailing stack: StcEcs-teamcityAgentWindows`

MikeB on slack helped me locate where this was happening.  This is where it's being lowercased. 

https://github.com/cloudtools/stacker/blob/1cb24aa1a596261d533a5dfc4536757a3743b6ab/stacker/context.py#L116

Suggested fix:   Lowercase the bucket,  but don't lowercase anything that isn't bucket related.. and keep the upper casing. 

Considerations would be existing stacks that are deployed and named with lower casing.  We should probably not change the case in an existing stack, but new stacks could use the upper case.

issue_title: CloudFront ForwardedValues is missing QueryStringCacheKeys property
issue_body:
As per latest docs: http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-cloudfront-forwardedvalues.html

The CloudFront ForwardedValues looks like this:
```
{
  "Cookies" : Cookies,
  "Headers" : [ String, ... ],
  "QueryString" : Boolean,
  "QueryStringCacheKeys" : [ String, ... ]
}
```

so ForwardedValues in troposphere, should probably include something like this:
```
'QueryStringCacheKeys': ([basestring], False),
```

issue_title: CodeBuild Artifacts NameSpaceType not Supported
issue_body:
Hi,

Just came across the Issue with CodeBuild Artifacts Property NameSpaceType. AWS Cloudformation gives me following error:

> Property validation failure: [Encountered unsupported properties in {/Artifacts}: [NameSpaceType]]

which means that Cloudformation only supports Property "NamespaceType" and not "NameSpaceType" according to the [AWS Documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-codebuild-project-artifacts.html). Can you please update this at your end.

Please let me know if you require more information.

Regards

issue_title: Support building against bundled libzmq for cython extensions?
issue_body:
I'd like to build a cython extension against pyzmq. In order to do this portably, I'd like to be able to link against the bundled libzmq when it is present. I think that all I need to do this is to have a portable way to get the libzmq library path (like ``get_includes()``)  and to keep the bundled libzmq's header files somewhere sensible in pyzmq when it is bundled, adding that path to the output of ``get_includes``.

I'm happy to try implementing this, but I want to make sure there is not a good reason to avoid this.

This comes up for me a lot because pip defaults to installing bundled copies of libzmq, which then conflict with a system built libzmq, but that might be a harder issue to get around. (On my own machine, I can always do ``pip install --no-use-wheel pyzmq``, but I don't always have control over how pyzmq is installed.

issue_title: asyncio socket sub never return
issue_body:
here is my code below~!

```python
import unittest
import zmq
import zmq.asyncio

import asyncio

class ZMQConnector(object):
    ctx = zmq.asyncio.Context()

    def __init__(self):
        self._socket = self.ctx.socket(zmq.SUB)
        self._socket.setsockopt(zmq.SUBSCRIBE, b'')
        self._port = self._socket.bind_to_random_port(ZMQ_BIND_IP)
        print(self._port)

    @property
    def port(self) -> int:
        return self._port

    @property
    def socket(self) -> zmq.Socket:
        return self._socket

    def clean_up(self):
        self._socket.unbind('{}:{}'.format(ZMQ_BIND_IP, self._port))
        self._socket.__del__()

    def recv(self):
        return asyncio.ensure_future(self._socket.recv())

    def recv_multipart(self):
        return asyncio.ensure_future(self._socket.recv_multipart())
class TestZmqConnector(unittest.TestCase):

    def setUp(self):
        import asyncio
        import time
        import zmq
        import zmq.asyncio
        zmq.asyncio.install()
        from JZquantWebsocket.zmqconnector import ZMQConnector

        self.connector = ZMQConnector()
        self.pub_socket = ZMQConnector.ctx.socket(zmq.PUB)
        self.pub_socket.connect('tcp://127.0.0.1:{}'.format(self.connector.port))



    def test_zmq(self):
        import asyncio
        connector = self.connector
        pub_socket = self.pub_socket
        async def send(connector, pub_socket):
            recv  = asyncio.ensure_future(connector.recv())
            send =  asyncio.ensure_future(pub_socket.send(b'hello'))
            dones, _ = await asyncio.wait([recv, send])
        asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(send(connector, pub_socket))

```

when i run the unittest above.sometimes the test could pass successfully sometimes it failed(not failed it just stuck into the loop, some of the futures never return .) This situation happen ocassionally.

I dig into the code and found `connector.recv()` this future never return sometimes. I don't know why and how to solve this. this test sometimes succeed sometimes stuck. It is really strange~!

Help~!

issue_body:
Is it possible to switch between two installed versions of MATLAB "on the fly?"

Hello,

wkhtmltopdf has [moved the downloads provider](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/wkhtmltopdf-general/wZwTUol3mXI/GNVeyrKDdqIJ) from SourceForge to gna.org. As mentioned in the linked message, all earlier downloads have been removed from SourceForge and you are strongly advised to migrate to the new URLs. The following files in your repository will need to be updated:

* .travis.yml

It is recommended to either subscribe to the [mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/wkhtmltopdf-general) or [Twitter feed](https://twitter.com/wkhtmltopdf) to receive updates on new releases and further changes.

Thanks,
wkhtmltopdf maintainer

issue_title: A compatibility table about raven and sentry server is needed
issue_body:
As far as I know:

- raven 5.3.1 is incompatible with Sentry 6.4.4 (this was encountered by me today)
- raven 5.0.0 is incompatible with Sentry 6.3.3 (reported in #467 )

It very confusing that the protocol in major version of both client and server are not compatible with each other, which is not very friendly to the users. Many company has been using sentry for a long time, old ravens lives in their legacy projects, upgrade them to the latest version is hard enough, let along trying to make out which version of raven should be used for their 6.x Sentry server.

Since raven is already not backward compatible, a table to tell which version of raven works with which version of Sentry is needed, to make life easier for us.

issue_title: Access to sys.argv can fail for embedded interpreters
issue_body:
The initialization of ``raven.base.Client`` accesses ``sys.argv`` in some cases. Unfortunately, this attribute is not set for embedded python interpreters (i.e. for python is started from C/C++), and an ``AttributeError`` is raised.
I propose to replace ``sys.argv`` by ``getattr(sys, 'argv', [])`` instead to handle that case gracefully.

issue_title: Drop support for Python 3.3
issue_body:
## Reasons for dropping

Python 3.3 went [EOL last month](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CPython#Version_history) and is no longer receiving security updates.

* Here's the pip installs for SoCo from PyPI for the last month,  showing low numbers for 3.3:
```
$ pypinfo --percent --pip soco pyversion
python_version percent download_count
-------------- ------- --------------
3.5              37.2%            823
3.6              29.0%            641
2.7              17.0%            377
3.4              15.3%            338
3.3               1.5%             33
2.6               0.0%              1
```
* pip 10 will deprecate Python 3.3 support, pip 11 won't support it (https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3796)
* Very little PyPI traffic (June 2016) https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3796 and even lower pip installs for the last month:
```
$ pypinfo --percent --pip "" pyversion
python_version percent download_count
-------------- ------- --------------
2.7              76.8%    488,818,554
3.6               9.9%     62,911,206
3.5               7.9%     50,014,064
3.4               4.0%     25,283,462
2.6               1.2%      7,848,827
3.3               0.1%        755,895
3.7               0.1%        400,727
3.2               0.0%         51,588
None              0.0%         19,451
2.8               0.0%            202
3.1               0.0%            169
```
* Allows the introduction of non-3.3 code without the concern of 3.3 failing
* Frees up resources on Travis CI for SoCo and everyone else

## Reasons for keeping

* Supporting 3.3 in addition to 2.7/3.4+ isn't as difficult as supporting 2.6 or 3.2

---

Anything else, for or against?

An alternative to dropping is deprecating: announcing it will be removed in a future version.

issue_title: Conflict with django_jinja
issue_body:
We upgraded from django-debug-toolbar 1.4 to 1.5 and ended up with an error that seems to break compatibility with django_jinja.

We received the same error as this Stack Overflow question:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/38569760/django-debug-toolbar-template-object-has-no-attribute-engine

Line 175 in `panels.templates.panel.py` raises:

``` python
Exception Value: 'Template' object has no attribute 'engine'
```

If you look at the asker's written solution, you'll notice that they patched line 175 by checking for the `engine` attribute on `self.templates[0]['template']` or to alternately look for a `backend` attribute (which exists and fulfills the same function in Jinja2).

This seems to have it's origin in the way that template dirs are grabbed, by pulling the engine info from an individual template:

``` python
        # Fetch context_processors/template_dirs from any template
        if self.templates:
            context_processors = self.templates[0]['context_processors']
            template_dirs = self.templates[0]['template'].engine.dirs
        else:
            context_processors = None
            template_dirs = []
```

In the following linked commit, you can see that debug_toolbar used to have a `get_template_dirs()` function that used `django.template.engine.Engine.get_default()` to get an engine and `engine.dirs` to pull in the template dirs. I haven't tested based on this commit, but I suspect that removing the call to `django.template.engine.Engine.get_default()` and pulling template dirs from an individual template caused the breaking change:
https://github.com/jazzband/django-debug-toolbar/commit/6390279a5eba1dc47fc12627204484fda67d3825#diff-f6bb86ee9199645f22f9b23d002ed707L24

The following seems to work and I would assume has a lower chance of breaking compatibility with other template engines.

``` python
from django.template.engine import Engine
engine = Engine.get_default()
engine.dirs # returns [] in my case
```

We encountered this using:

```
Django==1.10 (also tried with 1.9.9)
django_jinja==2.2.0
Jinja2==2.8
django-debug-toolbar==1.5
```

issue_title: Inform the user if auth goes wrong
exceptions move
issue_body:
As we currently have a ton of exceptions defined across different points in the SDK, I would want to move those into a single place: sdk_exception.py and from now on any exception should be defined there. This will make things easier to follow in my opinion.

I would also like to remove one of the exceptions as they basically do the same thing:

- UnsupportedOperation
- UnsupportedMethod

Unless we clearly define them and use them as per the definition, one of the should go. I vote for UnsupportedMethod to be removed.

If I do not hear anything against this I will proceed with PR :)

issue_title: Using serializer resource name for nested objects
Self Referential, ManytoMany, included on create?
issue_body:
We have a model that has a self reference back through itself using `through` and `through_fields`, i.e. 
```python
class Deck(models.Model):
 
    children = models.ManyToManyField(
        'self',
        symmetrical=False,
        related_name='parents',
        through='DeckLineage',
        through_fields=('parent', 'child'),
    )

class DeckLineage(models.Model):
    parent = models.ForeignKey(
        Deck,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        related_name='when_parent')
    child = models.ForeignKey(
        Deck,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        related_name='when_child')

```

and we are using the standard serializers, i.e. 
```python
class DeckSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = models.Deck
        fields = '__all__'
```

I'm trying to figure out how to craft a `POST` such that I can specify the `children` relationship in the post so that the `DeckLineage` table gets populated on POST.  Since we have to create these relationships manually in Django, I imagine I'll have to write some custom handler. In that case: what is the best practice for handling included relationships on POST like this?

issue_title: Autobatch bug for ConstantPlusX (?)
issue_body: I don't have a test case for this, but it appears (to me) that ConstantPlusX::autobatch_sig is incorrectly implemented. It calls s.add_node(c) for the constant "c" being added, but c is a dynet::real whereas add_node assumes an int. If I understand autobatch correctly, this means nodes adding different float values such as 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, would end up all autobatched together and incorrectly add just one of these to all values.

issue_title: Should ONNX have Dropout / request for FeatureDropout
issue_body:
If ONNX is intended to be an *inference* oriented framework, arguably it should not have Dropout at all (since this is a purely training-time construct).

If we decide we should keep Dropout, then I'd also like to request adding FeatureDropout to ONNX https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/master/torch/nn/_functions/dropout.py#L58

issue_title: Incosistent pad attribute on Pad and Conv
issue_body:
Let's make sure it's consistent with the rest of ops and current implementations.

It seems that Pad is right now [x_beg, x_end, y_beg, y_end]: https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/onnx/backend/test/case/node/pad.py
While Conv is [x_beg, y_beg, x_end, y_end]

I'd argue it's confusing and we should change it. I'm a bit biased toward Caffe2 which does [x_beg, y_beg, x_end, y_end] - shall we go with that? The good thing about it is that if you have symmetrical padding it's just list*2

@ebarsoum @yuanbyu
